characters.name: Althea Shen
characters.description: Althea ShenBiographical InformationGenderFemaleStatusAliveCharacter InformationNicknameTheaRelationshipsFamilyJin Shen(father)Mei-Li Shen(mother)Nicky Shen(younger sister)Ryan Shen(younger brother)Mia Yang(Maternal Cousin)Dennis Soong(husband)Chloe Soong (sister-in-law)Diane Soong (mother-in-law)Alfred Soong (father-in-law)PortrayalPortrayerShannon DangFirst AppearancePilotAlthea ShenisNicky's sister and the oldest ofJinandMei-Li Shen's children. She's newly engaged and on her way to planning her dream Chinese wedding. Upon Nicky's return toSan Francisco, Nicky is shocked to discover that her older sister Althea is preparing to marryDennis Soong, an old high school classmate of theirs. Later, once Althea and her siblings manage to get the mob off their backs, the Shens happily celebrate Althea's marriage and Nicky and Mei-Li formally reconcile.

characters.name: Dennis Soong
characters.description: Dennis SoongBiographical InformationGenderMaleStatusAlivePortrayalPortrayerTony Chung[1]First AppearancePilotDennis Soongis a main character in The CW'sKung Fu. He is portrayed byTony Chung. Dennis is the beloved son of the wealthy Soong family, Althea’s fiancé Dennis is coasting on being the hot and popular guy after growing out of his nerdy high school “mathlete” persona. He’s breezed through his 20's on his effortless charm, good looks and money, making him the perfect trophy husband for Althea. Dennis is utterly besotted by Althea, and he sees only smooth sailing ahead. But clashes between their families (and a few of their own long-buried secrets) threaten to ruin their perfect engagement and to tear them apart on their way to the altar.[1] To be added

characters.name: Evan Hartley
characters.description: Evan HartleyBiographical InformationGenderMaleStatusAliveCharacter InformationOccupationADARelationshipsFriendsAlthea ShenRyan ShenPortrayalPortrayerGavin StenhouseFirst AppearancePilotEvan Hartleyis a highly successful Assistant District Attorney who still has a soft spot for his first love,Nicky, when she returns home. Once she is back from theYunnan Province, Nicky reconnects with her ex-boyfriend Evan, whom she broke up with because she at the time had been feeling lost and pressured under all of her mother's expectations. Nicky then reveals to him that she is investigating a new Triad gang extending their operations into Chinatown, and hoping that Evan's connections as the city's Assistant District Attorney can help. Their reunion is interrupted by Evan's new girlfriend Sabine, to which Nicky takes poorly as she confides in a vision ofPei-Ling Zhangwho appears to console her and encourage her to reconcile with her family. Later, while Nicky and Mei-Li finally have it out, Nicky mentions that it was Mei-Li that pressured her to break up with Evan and micro-managed her life until she traveled to China.

characters.name: Henry Yan
characters.description: Henry YanBiographical InformationGenderMaleBirthdayMarch 10, 1994[1]StatusAliveCharacter InformationOccupationTai Chi InstructorChinese Art History Grad Student at UCSFPortrayalPortrayerEddie LiuFirst AppearancePilotHenry Yanis a Tai Chi instructor at the San Francisco Chinatown Community Center who also happens to be a Chinese Art History graduate student at UCSF. Henry is an expert in Ancient Chinese history and folklore, which ends up benefitingNicky Shen, as she is dealing with ancient Chinese supernatural forces. He trained in Wushu China for three years prior. Nicky and Henry first meet, and the two seemingly have instant chemistry. It is so strong that it stopped a random Asian Auntie in her tracks to have her comment on the good-looking pair. Later, Henry reveals to Nicky the fabled magical potential of theweaponthatZhilan Zhangnow has in her possession.

characters.name: Jin Shen
characters.description: Jin ShenBiographical InformationGenderMaleStatusAliveCharacter InformationOccupationRestaurant OwnerRelationshipsFamilyMei-Li Shen(wife)Nicky Shen(daughter)Althea Shen(daughter)Ryan Shen(son)PortrayalPortrayerTzi MaFirst AppearancePilotJin ShenisNicky Shen's father. He and his wife,Mei-Li, are restaurateurs whose secrets threaten to destroy their lives just as they deal with the return of their estranged daughter. Nicky returns to her home and estranged family inSan Francisco’s Chinatown where her father Jin welcomes her with open arms. Unfortunately, Nicky is quick to notice that Jin is sporting a black eye. While Nicky storms out, Jin convinces his daughter not to run away to parts unknown again.

characters.description: Joe HarperBiographical InformationAge20's[1]GenderMaleStatusAliveCharacter InformationOccupationGraphic ArtistLGBTQ+ Community OrganizerSocial ActivistPortrayalPortrayerBradley GibsonFirst AppearancePatienceJoe Harperis a graphic artist and LGBTQ+ community organizer/social activist in Oakland. He also serves as Ryan's love interest for Season 1.[1] To be added After she accidentally barges intoRyanon a date, Nicky and Joe meet, and she learns from Joe more about King Kwong and how he has been exploiting its workers for some time now.

characters.name: Kerwin Tan
characters.description: Kerwin TanBiographical InformationGenderMaleStatusDeceasedPortrayalPortrayerLudi LinFirst AppearanceLast AppearanceGuidanceTransformationKerwin Tanis heir to the billionaire Tan family fortune. He puts on a suave playboy act to the world but is driven by a deep hurt and antipathy towards his father. An erotically charged partnership withZhilanwill provide him with just the path to revenge (and vindication) he has been waiting for.[1] After Zhilan recalls her last conversation with Pei-Ling, two years before their fatal showdown, she learns that her uncle was secretly receiving money from Pei-Ling to raise her all along. Feeling betrayed, Zhilan angrily confronts him while revealing she was the one that murdered Pei-Ling before forcing him to tell her where his research on the weapons is located. Her uncle ends up revealing that the ancient text her father studied had been sold to the powerful Tan family in Taipei. Zhilan intercepts Kerwin Tan, one of the family's affluent sons, with a proposed alliance which he readily agrees to take her up on. Meanwhile, Zhilan and Kerwin lay in bed together discussing Zhilan’s past. She shares that after a childhood of surgeries, she’s used to pain. He asks if that’s where her anger comes from, and she says that it comes from betrayal. She notices scars on his arm that he claims are from a car accident. Kerwin then insists they get back to reading the scroll. But unfortunately, Zhilan is disappointed as he walks away. He is described as “dashing, handsome, physically fit, and impossibly charismatic”.[1]
characters.personality_section: He is described as “dashing, handsome, physically fit, and impossibly charismatic”.[1]

characters.name: Kwai Chang Caine
characters.description: Kwai Chang CaineYoungOlderAliasesgrasshoperRelativesThomas Henry Caine(father)Kwai Lin(mother)Danny Caine(half-brother)Kwai Chang Caine(grandson)AppearancesPortrayed byDavid Carradine(adult)Keith Carradine(younger)Radames Pera(child)Stephen Manley(youngest) Kwai Chang Caine(Chinese: 虔官昌; pinyin:Qián Guānchāng) is the main character of the 1972 television seriesKung Fu. He is a Shaolin master who travels through the American Old West to find his half-brother,Danny Caine. Caine has been portrayed byDavid Carradineas an adult,Keith Carradineas a younger Caine, Radames Pera as a child Caine, and Stephen Manley as the youngest Caine.

characters.name: Kwai Chang Caine
characters.description: Kwai Chang CaineYoungOlderAliasesgrasshoperRelativesThomas Henry Caine(father)Kwai Lin(mother)Danny Caine(half-brother)Kwai Chang Caine(grandson)AppearancesPortrayed byDavid Carradine(adult)Keith Carradine(younger)Radames Pera(child)Stephen Manley(youngest) Kwai Chang Caine(Chinese: 虔官昌; pinyin:Qián Guānchāng) is the main character of the 1972 television seriesKung Fu. characters.name: Liang Daiyu
characters.description: Liang DaiyuBiographical InformationGenderFemaleStatusDeceasedCharacter InformationOccupationShaolin WarriorRelationshipsFamilyUnnamed Grandfather(deceased)Nicky Shen(descendant)PortrayalFirst AppearancePilot(mentioned)Liang Daiyuis a legendary character  mentioned inThe CW'sKung Fu. According toHenry, Liang lived  many, many, years ago during the Tang Dynasty.  According to the myth about her, when all the men in the village went off to war, a band of raiders attacked. Daiyu grabbed hergrandfather’s swordname Sofyan, and single-handedly kept the raiders at bay. The next day, she began training the other women in the village. Pretty soon, they weren’t just protecting themselves and their homes, but turning the tide of the war. InGuidance, it was revealed that she is in fact an ancestor ofNicky Shenand one of the first warriors in the bloodline.

characters.description: Master PoAffiliationKwai Chang CaineAppearancesPortrayed byKeye Luke Master Pois one of  the main characters of the 1972 television seriesKung Fu. Master Po was a priest at the Shaolin temple in Hunan.  He had said his favorite student was Kwai Chang Caine.

characters.name: Mei-Li Shen
characters.description: Mei-Li ShenBiographical InformationGenderFemaleStatusAliveRelationshipsFamilyLiang Daiyu(ancestor)Jin Shen(husband)Nicky Shen(daughter)Althea Shen(daughter)Ryan Shen(son)Xue Mei (sister)Mia (niece)Unnamed ParentsPortrayalPortrayerKheng Hua TanFirst AppearancePilotMei-Li Shenis the mother ofNicky Shen. She and her husband,Jin, are restaurateurs whose secrets threaten to destroy their lives just as they deal with the return of their estranged daughter. When Nicky returns toSan Francisco, she and Ryan are both less than thrilled to see her back home, with Mei-Ling feeling especially resentful that Nicky chose to run away rather than to express her reservations about being set up for an arranged marriage. As Nicky and her siblings begin asking local businesses for more information about their father's attackers, they are accosted by armed goons who are easily dispatched by Nicky. To be added

characters.name: Mia
characters.description: Mia YangBiographical informationPlace of deathChina, Killed by XiaoPhysical descriptionGenderFemaleAppearancesPortrayed byVanessa Yao Mia Yangis the daughter of Mei-Xue and a cousin ofNicky Shen, and a descendent of both the Warrior and Guardian bloodlines. Her unique heritage as a hybrid of both Warrior and Guardian makes her extremely powerful and dangerous, which has put her in the sights Russell Tan and his network of mercenaries, who intends to exploit her power for his own ambitions. With great knowledge of Chinese history and the lore of Biange, her rebellious phase led her to escape Russell Tan's grasp and ultimately run into her mother's side of the family. Now, with Biange reappearing once more, and Nicky's own conflict against Russell Tan, she is forced to confront her true potential and destiny. After Russell's defeat, Mia relocates to Yunnan and trains at Nicky's former monastery. However, her Warrior-Guardian hybrid bloodline made her a target forXiao, who poisoned her with her spell. As a result, Mia dies, becoming the first victim of Xiao's spell.

characters.name: Nicky Shen
characters.description: Nicky ShenBiographical InformationGenderFemaleEye ColorDark brownHair ColorBlackStatusAliveCharacter InformationOccupationShaolin Warrior (3 years in monastery in China)EducationLaw Student  at Harvard(Formerly; dropped out)RelationshipsFamilyJin Shen(father)Mei-Li Shen(mother)Althea Shen(older sister)Ryan Shen(younger brother)Xue Mei (maternal aunt)Mia Yang(maternal cousin)Liang Daiyu(maternal ancestor)Pei-Ling Zhang(distant relative)Zhilan Zhang(distant relative)FriendsPei-Ling Zhang(mentor; deceased)Zhilan ZhangRomance(s)Henry Yan(boyfriend)Bo Han(ex-boyfriend)Evan Hartley(ex-boyfriend)EnemiesXiao(deceased)Russell Tan(deceased)PortrayalPortrayerOlivia LiangSeasons1,2,3First AppearanceLast AppearancePilotBeginningNicky Shenis the protagonist in The CW'sKung Fu. She is portrayed byOlivia Liang. A young Chinese-American woman who drops out of college, Nicky then goes on a life-changing journey to an isolated monastery in China, after a quarter-life crisis. When she returns to find her hometown overrun with crime and corruption, she uses her martial arts skills and Shaolin values to protect her community and bring criminals to justice — all while searching for the assassin who killed her Shaolin mentor and is now targeting her. Nicky escaped hermother's matchmaking plan and went to theYunnan Provinceto live at a monastery Nicky arrives at the monastery withPei-Lingafter escaping

characters.name: Ryan Shen
characters.description: Ryan ShenBiographical InformationGenderMaleStatusAliveRelationshipsFamilyJin Shen(father)Mei-Li Shen(mother)Althea Shen(older sister)Nicky Shen(older sister)Mia Yang(maternal cousin)PortrayalPortrayerJon PrasidaFirst AppearancePilotRyan ShenisNicky's brother and the youngest ofJinandMei-Li Shen. Upon her return toSan Francisco, Nicky's brother Ryan and mother Mei-Li are both less than thrilled to see her back home. To be added

characters.name: Ryan Shen
characters.description: Ryan ShenBiographical InformationGenderMaleStatusAliveRelationshipsFamilyJin Shen(father)Mei-Li Shen(mother)Althea Shen(older sister)Nicky Shen(older sister)Mia Yang(maternal cousin)PortrayalPortrayerJon PrasidaFirst AppearancePilotRyan ShenisNicky's brother and the youngest ofJinandMei-Li Shen. characters.name: Zhilan Zhang
characters.description: ZhilanBiographical InformationGenderFemaleHair ColorblackStatusAliveRelationshipsFamilyPei-Ling Zhang(Older sister)Unnamed Father (deceased)Unnamed Mother (deceased)Unnamed UncleRomance(s)Kerwin TanPortrayalPortrayerYvonne ChapmanDiana Tsoy(child)First AppearancePilotZhilan Zhang(chinese:張志蘭) is the main antagonist ofSeason 1, and a supporting and main character from Season 2 onwards. She is a cryptic woman with deep criminal ties and a mysterious connection to the Shaolin monastery whereNickytrained in Kung Fu. Her quest for power led her to murder Nicky’s mentor in order to get her hands onThe Sword of Liang Daiyu. Thus proving that she will be a determined and dangerous foe.  She's uninhibited, she's vile at times, she's alluring, but with all of that she's also really heartbreaking and very, very much human. She might be seen as a villain by other, but to her, she's also very much so the hero of her own story.[1]
